And in terms of how that structure of the engagement takes shape, this is something that, you know, going back to just applying our own learnings and evolving how we work and setting our customers up for success.
We have what we call kind of like a two phased engagement.
It starts with what we like to call a strategy sprint, essentially.
And this is an eight-week process where we're going through and developing a comprehensive strategy with you.
So we're going very deep on personas, on, you know, you'll have like a deep research and market insights in terms of jobs to be done and things of that nature.
We'll develop an editorial roadmap, deep competitive analyses in terms of competitive gaps and what we're finding.
We'll then start to do the, you know, of course, necessary like technical audit things of like the foundation of your SEO health and anything that we need to change there.

And then finally, as we go through that process and we're aligning on the different topics through that editorial roadmap, we'll start to actually look at and calibrate on a few pieces of content in that eight-week period.
And the purpose of this is it's a really helpful process for us to start to align with you on like, what does an A-plus piece of content look like and how did we get to that?
And how we get to that for each customer is going to be different.
Like there's going to be different things about your personas, the tone that you're...
He wants to set, you know, a multitude of things, right?
And so as we get that feedback, that essentially creates hypotheses for us to think about, like, okay, how would we address this?
How do we make this more repeatable?
Typically, what that means is we're playing around with these different inputs to see, can we address that effectively?
If we can't, what we do is we train writers to come in and, you know, do what needs to be done to get it to that level.
And so essentially there's always a human-in-the-loop aspect to this, but the more we can do that, the more we can find that repeatability and, you know, check that box, if you will.
That just is one of many reasons we can scale quality content.
So that strategy sprint goes for eight weeks and then follows into a 12-month engagement where it's, okay, now we're, like, really executing on this.
We set an initial three to six-month roadmap from that strategy sprint.
But from there, that doesn't mean that roadmap is set in stone.

So it's not like it's pure execution and no strategy.
